item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the original source has been lost. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Started off thinking this was going to be a bit dull. A kick ball field that abducts children. Oh my was I wrong. A fantastic fate twisting scip +1.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Okay. This was pretty great. I think the simplest, and least invasive thing with respect to crosslinking this article is to take advantage of the "30 GoI pileup" as others called it. Links from there to the CotBG Hub, MC&D Hub, and SH Hub. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

13-Theta: And I know you also identified a High Priest of the Church of the Broken God, a mid-level executive with Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd., and two Serpent's Hand cell leaders among affected personnel. I used to get these reports. So the other [REDACTED] decided one of us was in on it too.

To this:

13-Theta: And I know you also identified a High Priest of the Church of the Broken God, a mid-level executive with Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd., and two Serpent's Hand cell leaders among affected personnel. I used to get these reports. So the other [REDACTED] decided one of us was in on it too.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 480 / 16 = 30 times that it's happened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"(480) civilian children from around the world have been affected by SCP-1271"

I thought that only 16 people were "summoned" to the area from around the world and the rest were just attracted there by its secondary effects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"they disappear from the area via an unknown form of teleportation"

As opposed to disappearing via a known form of teleportation?

item.title: This one definitely got my vote
item.content:encoded: What a great article. It's not any kind of horror or gore going on but it's so thoroughly unsettling that I got chills. The idea of this place manipulating those people—very Indian burial ground curse sort of motif, I suppose, but how it comes across as something as innocent as a game of soccer…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is strange and disturbing in all the right ways. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP Foundation.

Waht r u doin?

SCP Foundation.

Stahp.

Item is so far out of control SCP Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the one 10 year old allowed to live on an Antartic research base went missing for a few hours.

Nah, that's just Timmy's weekly tea and luncheon with the empress.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Every continent? Every single one? You think someone would have taken note when the one 10 year old allowed to live on an Antartic research base went missing for a few hours.

Has anyone checked the tree-line for bodies? I think that would be an obvious angle of investigation and would be noted even if they found nothing.

Have non-Foundation towel-bearers been recoverd? It specificaly says no staff that were "secondary recruits", which sort of implies that civilians that stumbled in have been found. But if so, what happened to them?

That is all my nits, and none of them hurt my enjoyment of this in the slightest. Very well done. I'm left wanting to know more, in a good way, and that is one of my standards for up-voting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your off-the-cuff chat snippets are better than some folks tales.
I think you should give some serious consideration to posting them up for real, by which I mean not just in chat.
For the short ones, like this, maybe a hub page that you can keep building on as you go. Because I hope that you won't stop coming up with these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Check.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No harm done. I'm more surprised nobody caught it before now. There are tools for checking if blank pages have drafts on them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god.

This is… ahaha.

I… must admit this. This was a SCP I have made for a series of SCP tales we have been writing with my girlfriend for a while, and I had to put it into a SCP format. What better form to do it than on the page, and then take a screenshot of the preview page to have a legit looking SCP.

Now… I have misclicked on "post". Then I went to the chat and asked around how to delete a SCP, because I pretty much had no idea how to do so, since I never actually planned to write a SCP myself (I don't think I really am able so far to write a decent one). No answers.
Then I learned how to delete the scp AND HAD NO IDEA IT WOULD REMAIN THERE!

This is a bit embarassing, but here goes. I am very sorry if I messed up anything.

All hail the memetic emu!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the mystery surrounding this, especially since we still don't know what a couple of the positions do. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not really a big fan of the years and numbers of children referenced because then the article gets too firmly dated.

Otherwise I really like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno then. Maybe Esk just doesn't know much about cameras?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had that thought but the exact line is

Guards are to be equipped with high-powered cameras with telephoto lenses

so an interpretation of "high-powered" as "employing an ultra-telephoto lens" would be redundant. The only thing I can think of is huge sensor size leading to very high digital image resolution.

e: ypto

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think he was thinking of a high powered lens/zoom, which lets you take pictures of stuff from reaaally far away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nitpick: What is a "high-powered camera"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aaaand upvoted. Very nice article, that was my only problem with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reference changed. Your non-group is now safely returned to 231:)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my headcanon, they're not still around. They were a one-off group that did one horrifying thing and were then all killed. We're still trying to deal with what they did… or what we think they did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I don't want them to either. I honestly mostly just liked the name, and assuming they're still around, 1271 could logically be motivated to place someone in its ranks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As their original creator, I really don't want the Children to become a GOI. It was a name I came up with for 231 and was intended to only be associated with that SCP. My idea was that it was a one-off group who did something horrifying but were then wiped off the face of the earth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gives me chills.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my goodness. This is subtle. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP to 11-Gamma: "You're going to take your drugs and you're going to like them!" Heheheh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really fantastic. Just the thought of something so seemingly insignificant impacting the world in such a big way sends tingles up my spine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dibs.

EDIT: I will almost certainly regret this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to think that the game has nothing to do with the effect. It's just a reason for these people to stand in these places.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoever writes the memetic emu is teh winrar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god, it's so far out of control SCP Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm posting now because I just stopped laughing. I started laughing as soon as you posted this.

ALL HAIL THE MEMETIC EMU

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have only two things to say about this.

memetickému

ALL HAIL THE MEMETIC EMU

The subject is so far out of control SCP Foundation.

This thing is so far out of our control man you don't even know.

That is all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Results from google translate
- OMG CHECHENS

Object #: SCP-9912

Property Type: Secure

Special Procedures Celoochranné :: SCP-9912 was placed in a separate container for standard anomalous objects, 1m x 1m, the Department for Safe SCP objects in Area-7. To access the SCP must obtain permission from the fourth level of the project researcher. Since memetickému effect the reported object is direct experimentation and use is limited to D-supervisory personnel for two guards. For use outside the D-personnel need authorization from at least one O5.

The subject is so far out of control SCP Foundation.

Description :: SCP-9912 is a type of Philippine pocket knife "Balisong". It has a black handle and knife blade and handle are very shabby. The connection handle to the blade are the words "Stainless Steel". The bases grip is roughly engraved writing letters "L" and "H".

The knife was found in 1991 in Barcelona, ​​Italy between deferred props after the incident 9912-1, when he first caught the attention of SCP Foundation. Since then stored in Area-7, located in the █ █ █ █ █, █ █ █ █ █ █ █ █ █ █ █ █ █.

Anomalous characteristics of the object will be reflected when the nasčítanou longer time period (the shortest recorded time of 2 hours, the longest recorded time for the manifestation of the implications of the memetic effect █ █ █ hours) held in the hands of one person. If the object at one time held more than one person, all of this incubation period accumulates at once. After this incubation period manifests primary effect object. From this moment the holder / s referred to as SCP 9912 SCP-9912-01.

The primary effect of the object SCP-9912 is on the memetic base. SCP-9912-01 is subject to a drastic modification of the optic nerves, caused by dysfunction [REVISED] in the brain. From this point SCP-9912-01 sees all living beings in their surroundings as the originator of danger to his person. This is accompanied by aggressive states, similar to the response of the animal, zahnaného the corner, according to SCP-█ █ █ █ named as █ █ █ █ █ █ effect. In this state, the person using the SCP-9912 (no other type of weapon the primary effect during rallies unused) terms all living beings in their immediate vicinity (a radius of about 20 meters, + / - 0.5 m) until the property has SCP-9912th After termination of ambition or remove SCP-9912 SCP-hand from 9912-01 to lapse into a catatonic state from which so far none of the people affected by PCBs, but did not awaken. It is speculated that this is connected with the stop [REVISED] in the brain.

Due to the incident 9912-2, is currently studied secondary effect in the incubation period, the Foundation still undiscovered.

Incident 9912-1:

Barcelona, ​​Italy (03/19/1991) During the testing of amateur theater SCP-9912 by one of the actors used as a prop. The primary effect manifested during the dress rehearsal. 0 fatalities, 2 injuries. Agents successfully empowers the Foundation object.

Incident 9912-2:

Red Lake, Minnesota (12/04/2009) - A woman with SCP-9912 before the manifestation of the primary effect of SCP-9912 is used to attack two young boys. Woman sentenced for first-degree murder and threats to health. One fatality, one injury. No side effect of unobserved. Agents successfully empowers the Foundation object.

Incident 9912-3:

Prague, Czech Republic (15.09.2012) - [DETAILS REMOVED]. 12 fatalities, 33 injured. Masquerading as 200 kg of ammunition explosion in an apartment building. Foundation Agents were unable to seize the building.

Appendix - (09/16/2012): The subject property is so far outside the SCP Foundation. In the past, the SCP-9912 has been the subject of several priority interest Global Occult Coalition, when in 19 and 20 █ █ █ █ was subject to theft twice during the attack on Area-7. It is also possible that during 9912-3 the incident occurred [DETAILS REMOVED]. It is not impossible that throughout the GOK has been the subject property destroyed, or they could [DETAILS REMOVED].

I'm surprised at how good google translate is getting

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm. I suppose you're right. I just saw that many big names and had a knee-jerk reaction to dislike them, much like many people on site have expressed before with pointless cross-references and GoI namedrops in other articles. After rereading, I see that they for the most part make sense (still iffy on the Scarlet King), and have changed my mind from neutral to upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I…. I didn't downvote. But I don't think that the cross-reference is one of the "good ones," which hinders the article, so I didn't upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just really excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would have upvoted if it weren't for the unnecessary 231 reference.

What an incredibly silly reason to downvote. Not all cross-references are bad, and while you're free to vote whichever way you want, I'll thank you not to police the good ones. Just my opinion, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Children who are located, identified, and interviewed later claim to have no memory of their activities involving SCP-1271 and are unable to contribute to Foundation understanding of the mechanisms underlying SCP-1271.

To date, only 10% of recovered civilians have any memory of their experiences with SCP-1271.

Fix that. Otherwise, good article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dammit Snow, why are you so good at this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mentioned this in chat.

I couldn't let it go.

And so, micro tale.

Fifteen years later...
They return.

Allan shifted on home plate, eyeing the blue team member across from him. The air was still and muggy, the hot summer sun beating down on the field.

Everyone was here. Everyone from that day, fifteen years ago.

The bowler rolled the ball right at the plate, and Allan tensed. Every second of his life had been leading up to this moment.

He released an explosive kick, every muscle in his leg working to achieve this one perfect angle. With a pop, the rubber ball sailed into the air.

Allan ran as he had never run before, everything that had led to this moment flashing through his mind.

The house fire that forced him to move

First base.

The man who had knifed him outside his new home in Pennsylvania, forcing him to return to his home town for emergency medical treatment

Second base.

His car dying less than a mile from his old house

Third base. He felt the runner close behind him, felt the hot breath on his neck as he closed on home plate.

As he took the final step, he felt the ball accelerate behind him. He ducked and turned, feeling the rush of air as it shot past his shoulder. He slammed bodily into home plate, a sense of release and joy flooding him as he thudded into the dirt.

As his team ran onto the field, Allan cheered. The roaring, unfettered joy of both teams filled the park.

After fifteen years, the tie was broken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thought: The fact that it's an Indian burial ground has little to do with it. The local tribe simply recognized its significance and used it as a burial ground.

Also yeah this is like four kinds of amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to take a guess that this is basically an invocation to greatness for some ancient god thing, or at least the leftovers of an ancient religious rite that's still compelling the acts.

I think I have a pretty good guess where 2 of the kids go after a match that haven't been identified, and a conjecture on the final one.

No-longer-table-breaking collapsible!
Are these it?

SCP-1271-4/12: become sacrifices and are buried under the tree referenced in the chant.

SCP-1271-16: Either drops out of society or otherwise becomes completely non-notable, a non-person in the world?

Oh, forgot to add. I particularly enjoyed what happened with the barbiturates interview. There was a good chance that this was seeking out people with certain fates already, but it pretty clearly pushed that possibility aside.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And before it started getting usage elsewhere, the same could have been said about the CotBG.

Minor one-off cults have to start somewhere, and there is nothing else in the article referencing 231 at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it was created long enough ago during a period that something big was happening, it could have slipped by. Especially if it was tagged in such a way as to skip the lowest rated pages.

Would need to see the page history to know for sure though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really liked the second interview. There's just enough going on around this to keep things interesting, plus the effects are numerous and intriguing all on their own.

A couple things:
A couple things:

every continent

Even Antarctica? Do they come from the other world kingdom or something?

they disappear from the area via an unknown form of teleportation

I would say you don't need anything after "area". I could make all sorts of comments about what known types of teleportation are, how they even know it's teleportation in the first place, but the fact is, they just disappear.

And on that note, under the second effect, it says they've never recovered any affected Foundation personnel. Do these people all just vanish and never return? (Obviously not, judging by the effects list, but that's not obvious at first.) Just the towel boys? That needs to be made a little clearer.

Also, I don't think you need the full chant at the end. Just a translation will do fine, in my opinion.

The more I talk about this to myself though, the more I think I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, those were just off-handily mentioned, I believe in order to illustrate that this field has intentions beyond simply fucking with people's life. They were hardly the focus of the piece. Maybe the same effect could be achieved with normal organizations (U.N, government personnel, heads of industry), but this is the SCP verse, so you might as well use the resources you were given.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Scarlet King member, Broken God high priest,two of The Serpent's Hand, and a MC&D or two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What 30 GoI pileup? All you had there is one unaffiliated person, one member of a non-GoI cult, and a Foundation personnel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except for the 30 GoI pileup at the end, this was pretty good. Was there a reason you felt it necessary to namedrop so many? Seems a bit excessive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The CotSK have only ever appeared in 231, and in Second Watch, in which they were attempting the same thing. One really doesn't go without the other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not a 231 reference. CotSK is a very minor GOI (that we don't bother having an entry for, because of how minor they are), whose most notable accomplishment is 231. They're referred to in a number of other places, like the Horizon Initiative stuff. That's like calling a CotBG reference an 882 reference.

This is decent, though the Indian burial ground element feels poorly integrated with the rest of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, very mysterious and interesting.

ever since.

Minor tone issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would have upvoted if it weren't for the unnecessary 231 reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did find this entitled playing kickball with demons, apparently Native American:
https://www.sacredhooptrading.com/images/products/10012.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. One small problem:

one hundred twelve (126)

Found in the first addendum. Which one is it?

Other than that, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree entirely.

Very strange fate-based SCP. I like it a lot, very mysterious.
